home.card2Desc: Track schedules, submission windows, and official updates from the organizers.
home.card3Desc: Connect young thinkers interested in data, quantitative reasoning, and applied math.

faq.pageSubtitle: Rules and essential notes for contestants participating in a contest.
faq.adminPageSubtitle: Operational principles for accounts, contests, teams, submissions, and data.

userItems.q: What is a contestant account allowed to access?
userItems.a: A contestant account is only for organizer-approved actions: viewing profile data, checking assigned teams, following contests, and submitting during valid windows. Do not share the account outside your team.

userItems.q: What should I verify after receiving an account?
userItems.a: After signing in, check Profile and My Contests. If your name, email, school, team, or members are incorrect, contact the organizers before the submission deadline.

userItems.q: Why can I not edit my personal information?
userItems.a: Contestant data is managed by the organizers to keep team records consistent. Contestants can review and confirm details; official changes must go through the organizers.
Active means the contest is published and may have open stages. Closed means the contest has ended. Cancelled means it will not continue. Draft is organizer-only and not public.
Follow both the overall contest window and each stage. Submission permission depends on a stage that accepts submissions, not only on the contest's general status.
A submission is accepted only when you belong to the contest team or individual entry and the submission stage is open. After the deadline, the system blocks new uploads, replacements, and deletes.

userItems.q: Can I replace a submitted file?
userItems.a: Yes, as long as the submission stage is still open. The latest replacement is the file currently recorded for your team or individual entry.

userItems.q: What if I cannot see the prompt or submission form?
userItems.a: Check that you are signed in with the correct account, assigned to the contest, and in a stage that accepts submissions. If it is still missing, contact the organizers with your username and team code.

userItems.q: How do I contact the organizers?
userItems.a: Hotline: 0353 840 125 - Phan Minh Trang (Head Organizer). Email: contact.toanmohinh@gmail.com. Instagram: archive.toanmohinh. Facebook: Toán Mô hình Hà Nội.

userItems.q: What should I do before the final deadline?
userItems.a: Do not wait until the last minute. Confirm the file format, team, contest, and that the latest submission appears in My Contests.

Click Sign in in the navigation bar, enter the issued username and password, then confirm your account appears on the right. If sign-in fails, check for extra spaces or contact the organizers for a password reset.

userItems.q: How do I view my contests?
userItems.a: After signing in, open Profile and choose My Contests. This page lists assigned contests, team details, members, current stage, prompt, and latest submission.

userItems.q: How do I quickly find a team or contest?
userItems.a: In My Contests, use search for the contest name, team name, or team code. You can also filter by submission status to show contests with open submission windows.
Open My Contests, find the correct contest and active submission stage, choose a file, and submit. After submitting, check the submission area to confirm the latest file is recorded.

userItems.q: How do I replace or delete a submission?
userItems.a: While the submission stage is still open, use the replace or delete action in the contest's submission area. Once the stage closes, the system locks those actions.

adminItems.q: When should a contest be published?
adminItems.a: Move a contest to Active only after the title, slug, description, rules, participation type, team limits, time window, and stages have been reviewed. Draft contests are not public.

adminItems.q: What are the rules for stages?
adminItems.a: Each stage must sit inside the contest window, have a clear name, and start before it ends. Enable submissions only for stages that should actually receive work.

adminItems.q: How should rules and prompts be entered?
adminItems.a: Rules should describe the general contest policy. Stage prompts should live on the relevant stage so contestants see the right material in the right context.

adminItems.q: What are the team and member rules?
adminItems.a: Teams must respect the contest's min/max member settings. Leaders and members must be valid users, admin accounts should not be contestants, and a user should not be duplicated within the same contest.

adminItems.q: When should a team be edited?
adminItems.a: Edit a team only with clear operational approval. Changing the leader, members, or team code can affect contestant access and submission rights.
Contestants may replace submissions while the submission stage is open. After a stage closes, admins should review or download submissions through the management view and avoid direct data edits.

adminItems.q: What is each bucket used for?
adminItems.a: post-images stores public images for posts and contests. submissions stores uploaded work and should be accessed through signed URLs instead of public links.

adminItems.q: What should admins watch when managing accounts?
adminItems.a: Usernames should remain stable because they are used for team assignment. When resetting passwords or changing roles, confirm the recipient to avoid granting the wrong access.

adminItems.q: When should Admin Workspace data be checked?
adminItems.a: Use the read-only data tables for quick review of accounts, posts, tags, and operational state. For business changes, prefer the dedicated UI over manual data edits.

adminItems.q: What should happen before closing a contest?
adminItems.a: Review stage status, download or verify required submissions, make sure team changes are complete, then update the contest status according to the organizers' decision.

adminItems.q: How do I create a contestant or admin account?
adminItems.a: Go to Account Management, create a new account, and enter username, password, contact details, and role. After creation, share the credentials with the correct recipient through an internal channel.

adminItems.q: How do I create a complete contest quickly?
adminItems.a: Go to Contest Management, choose New contest, then enter general information, cover image, rules, participation type, member limits, and at least one stage. Keep it as Draft until review is complete.

adminItems.q: How do I add a team?
adminItems.a: In Contest Management, open Registrations for the target contest. Enter team code, team name, level, leader, and members, then save. If validation fails, recheck usernames and member limits.

adminItems.q: How do I download contestant submissions?
adminItems.a: Open the contest's registration/submission management view, expand the team, and download the file. Download links are signed URLs with an expiry, so generate a new link if one expires.

adminItems.q: How do I manage images and files in buckets?
adminItems.a: Open Bucket, choose post-images for public images or submissions for uploaded work. Admins can create folders, upload, rename, delete, and copy URLs.
